summary: A high‑quality soundboard capture of the Greek Theatre’s mellow September ’81 afternoon, highlighted by killer jams like Jack Straw > Bertha, Terrapin Station, Never Trust A Woman and a rolling Sweet Sugar Magnolia/Satisfaction sequence.
review: This SBD source finally gives the 13‑Sept‑1981 Greek show the clarity it deserves – the mix is clean overall, though Garcia’s guitar can sit a bit low at times. Musically the concert shines with an easy‑going first set and an epic second set packed with standout moments (Cold Rain > Samson, LS > ST, Terrapin Station, Never Trust A Woman, The Wheel, Miracle, GDTRFB, Sugar Magnolia and Satisfaction).

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently enthusiastic 5‑star reviews praise the performance; recording is praised as the best source despite minor mix quirks, yielding a strong combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: New SBD source fixes previous tinny, washed‑out issues; overall clear and balanced but Garcia’s guitar is occasionally buried. A few reviewers note occasional crowd noise (rain‑like) and minor mix quirks.

standout_songs: Goin' Down the Road Feels Bad
show_quality.setlist_flow: Mellow, sun‑lit first set builds into a high‑energy second set with seamless transitions and extended jams; the show feels like a relaxed afternoon that erupts into classic Dead fireworks.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for fiery slide work and strong vocals, though his guitar can be low in the mix and his voice sounded slightly off on some tracks.
band_member_comments.Phil: Earlier version washed out; on this night Phil was briefly “limping” but recovered to crush Never Trust A Woman.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboards) took charge after Terrapin, launching into Never Trust A Woman and adding vibrant textures throughout the set.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey’s beam‑drum work on ‘The Greek’ segment is highlighted as a powerful atmospheric moment.
